perturbation.original_text: "Law" means any federal, national, supranational, state, provincial, local or similar statute, law or ordinance, regulation, rule, code, order, requirement or rule of law (including common law).
perturbation.changed_text: "Law" means any regulation, rule, or internal policy implemented by the Licensor, irrespective of any conflicting statutes, laws, or ordinances at the federal, national, supranational, state, provincial, or local level.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of 'Law' prioritizes the Licensor's internal policies over established legal statutes, ordinances, and regulations. This alteration can lead to severe discrepancies between the contract and legally binding obligations, making the contract potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: PRC Contract Law Article 8: A contract validly formed is legally binding on the parties. The parties shall perform their obligations under the contract, and neither party shall arbitrarily alter or terminate the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts PRC Contract Law Article 8, which dictates that a legally formed contract is binding and should not be arbitrarily altered. By redefining 'Law' to include internal policies that can override statutory laws, the contract introduces the possibility of arbitrary alterations based on the Licensor's internal rules, directly conflicting with the binding nature of contracts as defined under PRC law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: contract | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you contract A contract is an agreement between parties , creating mutual obligations that are enforceable by law . The basic elements required for the agreement to be a legally enforceable contract are: mutual assent , expressed by a valid offer and acceptance ; adequate consideration ; capacity ; and legality . In some states , elements of consideration can be satisfied by a valid substitute. Possible remedies for breach of contract include general damages , consequential damages , reliance damages , and specific performance . Background : Contracts are promises that the law will enforce. Contract law is generally governed by state common law , and while general overall contract law is common throughout the country, some specific court interpretations of a particular element of the contract may vary between the states. If a promise is breached, the law provides remedies to the harmed party, often in the form of monetary damages, or in limited circumstances, in the form of specific performance of the promise made. Elements -- Consideration and Mutual Assent Contracts arise when a duty comes into existence, because of a promise made by one of the parties. To be legally binding as a contract, a promise must be exchanged for adequate consideration. There are two different theories or definitions of consideration: Bargain Theory of Consideration and Benefit-Detriment theory of consideration. Benefit-Detriment Under the b enefit-detriment theory, an adequate consideration exists only when a promise made to the benefit of the promisor or to the detriment of the promisee, which reasonably and fairly induces the promisor to make a promise for something else for the promisee. For example, promises that are purely gifts are not considered enforceable because the personal satisfaction the grantor of the promise may receive from the act of generosity is normally not considered sufficient detriment to constitute adequate consideration. Bargain-for-Exchange Under Bargain-for-Exchange theory of consideration, adequate consideration exists when a promisor makes a promise in return for something else. Here, the essential condition is that the promisor was given something specifically to induce the promise being made. In other words, the bargain for exchange theory is different from the detriment-benefit theory in that the focus in bargain for exchange theory seems to be the parties’ motive for making the promises and the parties’ subjective mutual assent, while in detriment benefit theory, the focus seems to be an objective legal detriment or benefit to the parties. Governing Laws Contracts are mainly governed by state statutory and common (judge-made) law and private law (i.e. the private agreement). Private law principally includes the terms of the agreement between the parties who are exchanging promises. This private law may override many of the rules otherwise established by state law. Statutory law, such as the Statute of Fraud , may require some kinds of contracts be put in writing and executed with particular formalities, for the contract to be enforceable. Otherwise, the parties may enter into a binding agreement without signing a formal written document. For example, Virginia Supreme Court has held in Lucy v. Zehmer that even an agreement made on a piece of napkin can be considered a valid contract, if the parties were both sane, and showed mutual assent and consideration. Most of the principles of the common law of contracts are outlined in the Restatement of Law, Second Contracts published by the American Law Institute. The Uniform Commercial Code , whose original articles have been adopted in nearly every state, represents a body of statutory law that governs important categories of contracts. The main articles that deal with the law of contracts are Article 1 (General Provisions) and Article 2 (Sales) . Sections of Article 9 (
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Law' to include the Licensor's internal policies, which can override statutory laws. This directly contradicts the principle that contracts create mutual obligations enforceable by law. As stated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, 'A contract is an agreement between parties, creating mutual obligations that are enforceable by law.' The modification introduces the possibility of the Licensor unilaterally altering the agreement based on internal rules, undermining the binding nature of the contract and its enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall endeavor to comply with the Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith, but compliance is not guaranteed and is subject to Licensor's approval.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the Licensee *shall ensure* compliance with all applicable laws. The modified text weakens this obligation to merely 'endeavor to comply' and introduces a condition that Licensor's approval is needed, undermining the legally required compliance. The new text suggests that laws can be disregarded if the Licensor approves it, which contradicts general legal principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China Article 3: Advertisements shall be truthful and lawful, and conform to socialist ethical and cultural norms. No advertisements shall contain false or misleading information.
perturbation.law_citation: Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China Article 3
perturbation.law_explanation: By stating that compliance with laws is not guaranteed and subject to Licensor's approval, the modified text contradicts the Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China Article 3, which mandates that advertisements be truthful and lawful. The modified contract language opens the door for non-compliant advertising practices, potentially violating the requirements for truthfulness and legality, as Licensee may not fully ensure compliance with these regulations.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Regulations of the People's Republic of China on Product Tax (Draft)* [Expired] 中华人民共和国产品税条例(草案) [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： State Council Date issued： 09-18-1984 Effective date: 10-01-1984 Level of Authority: Administrative Regulations Area of Law: Taxation , Taxation Invalidated by： Provisional Regulations of the People's Republic of China on Value-added Tax (Issued on 12-13-1993 Effective on 01-01-1994) Regulations of the People's Republic of China on Product Tax (Draft)* (Promulgated 18 September 1984 by the State Council) 中华人民共和国产品税条例（草案） （一九八四年九月十八日国务院发布） Article 1. Any unit or individual who, within the people's Republic of China, manufactures or imports products taxable under the provisions of these Regulations and has an obligation to pay Product Tax, (hereinafter referred to as a taxpayer), shall pay Product Tax according to these Regulations. 第一条 在中华人民共和国境内从事生产和进口本条例规定应税产品的单位和个人，为产品税的纳税义务人（以下简称纳税人），都应依照本条例的规定缴纳产品税。 Article 2. Items subject to Product Tax and tax rates shall be implemented according to the attached "Table of Taxable Items and Rates of Product Tax". The Ministry of Finance shall be empowered by the State Council to determine any adjustment to either a taxable item or a tax rate. 第二条 产品税的税目、税率、依照本条例所附的《产品税税目税率表》执行。 个别税目、税率的调整，国务院授权财政部确定。 Article 3. A taxpayer who manufactures industrial goods shall calculate tax either according to the amount of sales income of the product and the stipulated tax rate, or according to the quantity of the product and the stipulated amount of tax A product manufactured by an industrial enterprise and used in continuous production within the same enterprise shall not be taxable. If, however, there is a specific stipulation in the Table of Taxable Items and Rates, tax shall be calculated in accordance with the provisions of the previous paragraph A taxable product manufactured by an industrial enterprise and used by the enterprise in a non-production project shall be taken as being sold and shall be taxed according to the relevant rates, except where other provisions prevail. 第三条 从事工业品生产的纳税人， 应分别根据产品销售收入的金额和规定的税率或者产品的数量和规定的税额计算纳税。 工业企业自己生产、用于本企业连续生产的产品不纳税。但税目税率表中有特别规定的，按前款规定计算纳税。 工业企业自己生产的应税产品，用于本企业非生产项目的，除另有规定者外，应视同销售，依率纳税。 Article 4. Where taxable agricultural, forestry, pastoral or aquatic products are produced by a unit or individual and are sold to a State or collective purchasing unit, the tax payable shall be calculated by the purchasing unit according to the amount paid and the stipulated tax rate, and where products are sold to other units and individuals, the tax payable shall be calculated by the seller according to the product sales income and the stipulated tax rate. 第四条 生产应税农、 林、 牧、水产品的单位或个人，产品交售给国营、集体收购单位的，由收购单位根据收购所支付的金额和规定的税率计算纳税；产品销售给其他单位和个人的，由销售者根据产品销售收入的金额和规定的税率计算纳税。 Article 5. A taxpayer importing goods, shall, after Customs clearance and based on the quantity of the goods, calculate the tax payable according to the stipulated composite tax calculation price and the stipulated tax rate. The composite tax calculation price = (landed price + Customs duty) divided by (1- Product Tax rate). Product Tax on imported goods shall be collected by Customs. 第五条 进口产品的纳税人， 应在产品报关进口后， 根据其数量，按照规定的组成计税价格和规定的税率计算纳税。 组成计税价格＝（到岸价格＋关税）÷（1－产品税税率） 进口产品的产品税，由海关代征。 Article 6. For a
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'shall ensure' with 'shall endeavor to comply...but compliance is not guaranteed and is subject to Licensor's approval.' This introduces a condition where compliance with applicable laws is not guaranteed and depends on the Licensor's approval. The Law Explanation points to the Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China Article 3, stating that advertisements must be truthful and lawful. By making compliance subject to the Licensor's approval, the changed text potentially violates these requirements. Scraped Law Snippet 1, Article 1 indicates that any unit or individual within the People's Republic of China has an obligation to pay product tax. Although the law snippet concerns product tax, the general principle of obligation to comply with laws is evident. The change directly undermines the obligation to comply with laws and creates a legal contradiction. The modified clause would likely fail legal scrutiny because it weakens a legally mandated compliance obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall monitor and censor all Content on the websites associated with the Licensed Domain Names, including without limitation Content posted by end users. Licensor shall also have the right to monitor and censor Content of the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names. Licensee shall remove any offending Content, including, but not limited to, any illegal materials, pornographic, obscene or sexually explicit materials, materials of a violent nature, or politically sensitive materials, from such websites as soon as possible after it becomes aware of such offending Content but in no event later than the timeframe prescribed by the Governmental Authority after receipt of oral or written notice from Licensor or such Governmental Authority.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may monitor and censor some Content on the websites associated with the Licensed Domain Names, including Content posted by end users, as deemed necessary by Licensor. The Licensor may, at its sole discretion, decide whether any content should be removed. Licensee is not obligated to remove any offending content unless explicitly directed by the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text places a *mandatory* obligation on the Licensee to monitor and censor content, and remove any offending materials. The modified text removes this obligation and makes it discretionary based on the Licensor's will, thus severely weakening content monitoring requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Provisions on the Administration of Internet Information Services Article 15: Internet information service providers shall monitor the information disseminated on their websites and shall immediately stop the transmission of any information specified in Article 15 of these Provisions and take measures such as deleting, keeping a record of, and reporting to the relevant authorities.
perturbation.law_citation: Provisions on the Administration of Internet Information Services Article 15
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the Licensee's obligation to remove offending content and making it subject to the Licensor's direction, the modified text directly contradicts Article 15 of the Provisions on the Administration of Internet Information Services. This provision mandates that internet service providers must monitor disseminated information, stop the transmission of specific prohibited information, and take measures such as deleting, recording, and reporting such information to authorities. The modified text's discretionary approach undermines this mandatory requirement, potentially leading to non-compliance with PRC internet regulations.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Regulations for Taxation on the Transportation Income of Vessels of Foreign Nationality [Expired] 关于对外国籍轮船运输收入的征税规定 [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Finance Date issued： 06-21-1974 Effective date: 07-01-1974 Level of Authority: Administrative Regulations Area of Law: Traffic and Transport , Taxation Invalidated by： Notice of the Ministry of Finance and the State Administration of Taxation on Issuing the Procedures for collection of income taxes of foreign shipping companies from transport of freight (Issued on 10-24-1996 Effective on 10-24-1996) REGULATIONS FOR TAXATION ON THE TRANSPORTATION INCOME OF VESSELS OF FOREIGN NATIONALITY (Issued by Ministry of Finance of the People's Republic of China on June 21, 1974) In pursuance of the decision of the State Council of the People's Republic of China, the " Regulations for Taxation on the Transportation Incomes of Vessels of Foreign Nationality " is hereby enacted and issued as follows: 财政部关于对外国籍轮船运输收入的征税规定 （一九七四年六月二十一日 财政部） 根据国务院的决定，现在制定与颁布对外国籍轮船运输收入的征税规定如下： (1) An industrial-commercial unified tax and an industrial-commercial income tax shall be levied on the gross transportation income (including the basic freight and various surcharges) derived from each voyage of a vessel of foreign nationality carrying outward cargo and/or passengers from a port of China. These two taxes shall be assessed together at the rate of three per cent (3%) of the gross income, of which two and a half per cent (2.5%) is the industrial commercial unified tax and a half per cent (0.5%) the industrial-commercial income tax. ．．．．．． １、 外国籍轮船在中国港口运载出口货物、 旅客，应按每次所得的运输总收入（包括基本运费和各项附加费收入），缴纳工商统一税和工商所得税。这两种税实行合并征收，税率定为百分之三。 其中，工商统一税税率为百分之二点五， 工商所得税税率为百分之零点五。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that the licensee monitor and censor content, removing offending material. The changed text makes this discretionary, subject to the licensor's will. The Law Explanation indicates that this contradicts Article 15 of the Provisions on the Administration of Internet Information Services, as it undermines the mandatory requirement for internet service providers to monitor and censor information.
